summary: Excellent soundboard source with crisp audio; the show shines thanks to a monster Shakedown, masterful Masterpiece, and a stellar Picasso Moon → Fire sequence.
review: The gd93‑05‑27.sbd.miller recording is widely praised for its clear, well‑balanced sound – many reviewers call it “excellent” or note that it’s the best version of this show on the net. Musically the concert delivers a solid first set highlighted by Shakedown Street, Dire Wolf and When I Paint My Masterpiece, while the second set offers peaks such as Picasso Moon into an outstanding Fire on the Mountain and the unique Cassidy → Uncle John’s Band → Cassidy segue; the only real blemish is the widely‑criticised Wave to the Wind.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈9/12) give 4–5 stars and praise both performance and sound quality; only a few note specific song weaknesses, so the combined show‑and‑recording score lands in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the audio as crisp, excellent and superior to previous releases; a couple note a slightly imperfect mix on later tracks and some compression in the summer‑93 era sound.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is consistently strong with a high‑energy opener and tight jams; second set swings between highs (Picasso Moon → Fire, unique Cassidy segue) and lows (the widely‑panned Wave), ending on an uplifting Gloria.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is repeatedly called “masterpiece” level, with a soaring solo on Masterpiece and tight interplay on Fire.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is described as thumping, resonant and providing a solid foundation throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards are praised – Brent’s era vibe noted, Vince’s Toccata outro on drums highlighted; overall keyboard work deemed great.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drumming is called “very nice” with an especially strong build into the Uncle John’s Band segue.
